title: Find a Bug with git bisect
description: Run a git bisect session to identify the commit that introduced a regression, then recover a lost commit using reflog.
starterCode:
# Task: Use git bisect to find a regression, then use reflog to recover
#
# Part 1 — Bisect:
# 1. Start a bisect session
# 2. Mark HEAD as bad (broken)
# 3. Mark commit abc1234 as the known-good commit
# 4. Simulate marking the next checkout as bad
# 5. End the bisect session
#
# Part 2 — Reflog recovery:
# 6. Show the reflog
# 7. Reset hard to HEAD@{2} to recover the pre-bisect state

# Write your commands below:
solutionCode:
# Part 1 — Bisect session
git bisect start
git bisect bad
git bisect good abc1234
# Git checks out midpoint — simulate testing and marking bad
git bisect bad
# End the bisect and restore HEAD
git bisect reset

# Part 2 — Reflog recovery
git reflog
git reset --hard HEAD@{2}
hints: git bisect start initializes the bisect session — always the first command
git bisect bad with no argument marks the currently checked-out commit as broken
Provide the known-good SHA or ref: git bisect good abc1234
After each Git-chosen midpoint, test and mark good or bad; Git will narrow down
git bisect reset returns you to the original branch and ends the session
git reflog shows HEAD@{0}, HEAD@{1}, HEAD@{2} etc. — recent positions of HEAD
git reset --hard HEAD@{N} moves HEAD and the working tree to that reflog entry

validationRules.errorMessage: Start the bisect session with git bisect start
Mark a commit as bad with git bisect bad
Mark the known good commit abc1234 with: git bisect good abc1234
End the bisect session with git bisect reset
Use git reflog and git reset --hard HEAD@{N} to recover

simulatedOutput.success:
Bisecting: 511 revisions left to test after this (roughly 9 steps)
[m3n4o5p6] Checkpoint: halfway through history

Bisecting: 255 revisions left to test
[q7r8s9t0] Earlier checkpoint

git bisect reset: HEAD is now at a1b2c3d4 Latest commit

reflog output:
HEAD@{0} a1b2c3d reset: moving to HEAD@{2}
HEAD@{1} e5f6g7h8 bisect: checkout midpoint
HEAD@{2} a1b2c3d commit: Add login feature
HEAD@{3} 9i0j1k2l commit: Fix tests

Reset complete. HEAD is now at a1b2c3d Add login feature
simulatedOutput.failure: ERROR: Make sure to run git bisect start, git bisect bad, git bisect good abc1234, git bisect reset, and use git reflog with git reset --hard HEAD@{N}.
